name: Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region (XUAR)
regulatory_basis: US UFLPA (rebuttable presumption); CBP WROs; UK MSA enforcement; EU CSDDD risk-based DD; SC sanctions designations
note: Goods produced wholly or in part in XUAR are presumed inadmissible into US under 19 USC §1307.

name: Mae Sot, Thailand (Myanmar border)
regulatory_basis: ILO Better Work Thailand findings; Verité 2018+ assessments
note: Migrant Burmese workers, informal sub-contracting, documented passport retention and recruitment-fee debt bondage.

name: Sialkot, Pakistan
regulatory_basis: ILO IPEC findings; FIFA/Adidas/Nike sourcing commitments post-1996
note: Historic child-labor in soccer-ball stitching; significant reform but residual sub-contracting risk.

name: Katanga + Kolwezi + Likasi, DRC
regulatory_basis: OECD DD Guidance for Minerals; EU Conflict Minerals Reg (2017/821); Amnesty International 2016+ reports
note: Artisanal small-scale mining (ASM) sites with child labour adjacent to industrial mines; unverified chain-of-custody.

name: Ashulia + Narayanganj, Bangladesh
regulatory_basis: Bangladesh Accord on Fire and Building Safety (post-Rana Plaza 2013); ILO Better Work Bangladesh
note: Concentration of RMG factories with documented Rana Plaza legacy + ongoing union-organising suppression.

name: Gulf states (kafala sponsorship system)
regulatory_basis: UN Special Rapporteur on Migrants reports; Qatar 2019 reforms (partial); ILO Better Work Jordan
note: Sponsor-tied employment status restricts mobility; passport retention historically endemic; reforms underway but enforcement uneven.

name: Cocoa belt — Côte d'Ivoire + Ghana
note: NORC 2020 study found 1.56M children working in cocoa in CDI+GH; EUDR requires plot-level geolocation from Dec 2024.

name: Thai fishing fleet + processing — Samut Sakhon + Ranong
regulatory_basis: EU IUU yellow card 2015-2019; ILO C188; US CBP WRO 2024
note: Migrant Burmese/Cambodian workers; documented vessel-trapping and shore-peeling-shed lock-ins.

name: Brick kilns — Bihar/UP India + Punjab Pakistan + Nepal
regulatory_basis: ILO IPEC studies; Walk Free GSI
note: Peshgi advance-debt system enrols whole family into seasonal bondage; intergenerational debt common.

name: Uzbekistan + Turkmenistan cotton
regulatory_basis: Cotton Campaign monitoring; ILO Better Cotton verification 2022+
note: Uzbekistan historically state-compelled cotton harvest; ILO 2022 declared free of systemic forced labour. Turkmenistan remains under boycott.
